builtin_modules: fs, path, os, http, https, crypto, stream, events, child_process, util, url, buffer, assert, zlib, net, tls, dns, readline, cluster, worker_threads, v8, vm
language_rules: require() and import both count as usage
Side-effect imports count as usage: `import 'dotenv/config'` means dotenv IS used

Dynamic patterns to flag:
  require(`${var}`)
  import(variable)
  require(array[i])
devDependencies: used in *.config.{js,ts} or test*/ or __tests__/ → devDependency usage
peerDependencies: do not flag as unused

Plugin packages are used via config file string references, NOT import statements:
  babel-plugin-*, @babel/plugin-*, @babel/preset-* → babel.config.js / .babelrc
  eslint-plugin-*, eslint-config-* → .eslintrc / eslint.config.js
  webpack loaders (*-loader) → webpack.config.js
  jest-*,  @jest/* → jest.config.js
  vite-plugin-*, rollup-plugin-* → vite.config.js / rollup.config.js

@types/* packages are used implicitly by TypeScript and never appear in import statements — check tsconfig.json compilerOptions.types or the presence of .ts files; if the project has TypeScript source files, treat @types/* as potentially used unless tsconfig explicitly excludes it
Packages listed in the 'scripts' field of package.json (e.g. 'jest', 'eslint', 'ts-node') are used as CLI tools and should be considered used even without an import statement
